item.title: The United States’ OpenAI Equity Stake, Meta The Neocloud, Karp’s Attack
item.description: Focus: Ahead of the holiday weekend, here’s what’s happening and what to look out for.

item.title: NVIDIA Unlocks AI Compute at Scale, Inviting Partners to Power the AI Infrastructure Buildout
item.description: Focus: As AI moves from model development to production inference, compute demand is accelerating and shifting toward continuously operating AI factories that generate tokens at scale. This shift requires access to large‑scale, multi‑tenant accelerated computing that can come online quickly, stay hi

item.title: Autoresearch: The feedback loop behind self-improving agents
item.description: Focus: Introspection co-founder Roland Gavrilescu explains autoresearch, agent “recipes,” self-improving loops, and why humans remain central to the software factory.

item.title: The website of the future may assemble itself for every visitor
item.description: Focus: Adobe is experimenting with “agentic sites” that generate pages around an individual user’s intent. At AIEWF, we talked to Carlos Sanchez about the Web's future.

item.title: Skill engineering and the case against one-shot AI design
item.description: Focus: Paul Bakaus talks to us about Impeccable, human judgment in a 'loopmaxxing' era, and why agents still need people to steer them.

item.title: AIEWF Daily Dispatch: Autoresearch and the tension between AI and human agency
item.description: Focus: The software factory vision met resistance today from speakers defending human understanding and control.

item.title: Joyride Through July With 12 Games Coming to GeForce NOW
item.description: Focus: Summer is heating up — and GeForce NOW is taking players along for the ride. Start the month with Monopoly: Star Wars Heroes vs. Villains, bringing a galaxy far, far away to the iconic board-game franchise, alongside 12 new games joining the cloud this month. Plus, don’t let the sun set on th

item.title: Image Generation and Visual Intelligence with Black Forest Labs
item.description: Focus: How has AI image generation evolved from blurry outputs to powerful visual intelligence models? Dustin Podell, Co-Founder and Researcher at Black Forest Labs, explains the progression from diffusion to flow matching, how modern image models work, and how they're being used for image editing a

item.title: Routing rules now available on AI Gateway
item.description: Focus: Vercel AI Gateway now supports routing rules . Routing rules are firewall-style rules that control which models your team can use, applied at the gateway level instead of in your application code. When a model goes down or gets retired, you usually have to ship a code change to move off it. W

item.title: Run any Dockerfile on Vercel
item.description: Focus: You have a server in a container. Maybe it's a Go service, a Rails app, a Spring Boot API, or a web server behind nginx. It speaks HTTP. It listens on a port. It just needs somewhere to run. Add a Dockerfile.vercel file to your project, and Vercel builds, stores, deploys, and autoscales the i

item.title: SkillOpt: Agent skills as trainable parameters
item.description: Focus: AI agents often fail because their instructions, or skills, are manually modified with no guarantee of improvement. Learn how SkillOpt turns skill editing into a training process, making agent behavior more reliable without changing model weights. The post SkillOpt: Agent skills as trainable
